glossary.title: Glossary
glossary.description: Key terms you'll encounter throughout the game.

terms.def: The resource spent to play cards or trigger abilities. If you don't have enough mana, you can't play that card.
The distance in tiles between two targets. "Within range N" means the effect only applies to targets within N tiles.
An effect that reduces a target's HP. If a wizard's HP reaches 0, that player loses.
Restores lost HP. Healing never raises HP above its maximum.
Taking a card from your deck into your hand. You usually draw a card at the start of each turn.
Sending a card from your hand to the graveyard. Discarded cards may be reused from the graveyard.
The cards you currently hold and can play.
Your pile of undrawn cards. You draw cards from here.
A card that triggers automatically from the catastrophe deck when your own deck runs empty. It has a powerful, board-shaking effect.
A card installed on the board that produces an ongoing effect. It stays on its tile and acts each turn.
Placing a ritual onto a specific tile on the board. An installed ritual provides an ongoing effect.
Removing a card from the game entirely instead of sending it to the graveyard. Burned cards can never be used again.
Relocating your wizard to another tile on the board. Position affects your range and damage.
The avatar you control on the board. If your wizard's HP reaches 0, you lose.
One player's phase of playing cards, moving, and so on before passing play. End your turn to hand play to your opponent.
The chance at the start of the game to swap an unwanted opening hand for new cards.
Where discarded or used cards collect. Some effects reuse cards from the graveyard.
